title: Triticum tauri
intro:

Given the lack of information on Triticum tauri, I can provide information on wheat, which is a cereal plant (Triticum spp.). 

Here is the information for Triticum aestivum (wheat):

Wheat is a type of cereal grass belonging to the genus Triticum, characterized by its edible grains.

Wheat has been associated with several health benefits, including lowering the risk of cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, and certain types of cancer. It is also a rich source of fiber, which can promote digestive health and support healthy blood sugar levels.

The bioactive constituents of wheat include various phenolic compounds, such as ferulic acid, sinapic acid, and coumarins, as well as other phytochemicals like phytic acid and avenanthramides.

Wheat is commonly used in various herbal preparations, including wheat grass juice, wheat germ oil, and wheat bran supplements.
taxonomy: Triticum tauri is actually an error in classification and it is now known as Triticum durum, however, it is sometimes considered a subspecies of Triticum aestivum. Triticum tauri is an extinct species of wheat, but some sources still consider it to be the same as Triticum durum. Triticum durum is a species of wheat that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is known for its high protein content. The classification of Triticum tauri is complex due to its historical use in different contexts.
parts: Triticum tauri, also known as wild emmer, is an ancient species of wheat that contains various medicinal properties.
